title: Venture capital & portfolio marketing
description: Practical GTM help for VC-backed founders. Short diagnosis sessions, portfolio workshops, and clear next steps before you scale spend or hiring.
buyerProblem: Portfolio teams often need senior commercial judgment early. Many founders are post-PMF but still founder-dependent on growth decisions.
usefulWhen: A founder wants a second opinion on the real bottleneck — not another channel plan.
The portfolio needs a practical session on AI-native GTM without tool hype.
A company needs a sharper growth story before the next fundraise or key hire.
growthSystem: Portfolio workshops on acquisition rhythm and reporting
90-day roadmap reviews tied to one primary constraint
Clean referral into sprint or fractional CMO work when it is a fit
proof: Mentoring across Google for Startups, Techstars, GrowthMentor, and founder education programmes
Operator-side support for teams moving from traction to repeatable acquisition

readingBlocks.heading: What this page is for
paragraphs: This is the VC and portfolio lens on the same WSS work: name the bottleneck, build the acquisition system, transfer the weekly rhythm.
If you are an investor or platform lead, think of this as senior GTM judgement for founders who are already shipping — not a generic marketing retainer.

readingBlocks.heading: Key takeaways
bullets: Start with the constraint, not a channel shopping list.
Use one weekly decision view so experiments stop colliding.
Keep proof and messaging aligned before you scale paid or outbound.
When the team is ready, hand off a running cadence — not a slide deck.

readingBlocks.heading: How a first engagement usually runs
paragraphs: We run a focused diagnosis, then agree whether a sprint, system build, or fractional CMO layer is the honest next step.
No forced long retainer — the goal is clarity and a system the team can run.
